spec_match.rationale: The page now listens on both beforeinput and input, with the default path logging `input.dataTransfer` for insertReplacementText after the native replacement lands.

interactive.rationale: Contenteditable with deliberate misspellings, track-changes toggle, diff inspector, log, restore + clear controls. Real interactivity.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept and feature blurbs now frame beforeinput as the contrast path and the final input event as the v143 payload.

edge_cases.rationale: Uses getTargetRanges() to recover the replaced range, falls back to startContainer slicing, and logs null input.dataTransfer as the pre-v143 path. Track-changes spans still do not survive all subsequent edits.

references.rationale: Page contains chromestatus.com/feature/6715253274181632 and the input-events spec.

design_system.rationale: Variables only; .rejected / .accepted styling uses accent colours.

openQuestions.title: Make track-changes survive subsequent edits
openQuestions.detail: If the user accepts a suggestion, then backspaces, the .rejected / .accepted spans get partially destroyed. Either mark them contenteditable=false (so they delete as units) or attach a MutationObserver that re-wraps. As-is the track-changes mode is fragile.

openQuestions.title: Compare contenteditable vs textarea for spell-check
openQuestions.detail: Spell-check works in textareas too, but the dataTransfer behaviour differs: contenteditable gets the new v143 input-event population, textarea does not. A side-by-side panel would teach the scope limit.

openQuestions.title: Surface an autocorrect-specific test (OS-level)
openQuestions.detail: macOS's autocapitalise / autocorrect also fires insertReplacementText but with no UI cue. A 'try typing 'i think' (lower-case) — macOS will fix it' note would broaden the demo beyond right-click-spelling.

summary: Solid spell-check inspector with side-by-side beforeinput/input handling. The default flow now demonstrates v143 input.dataTransfer; remaining work is focused on track-changes durability and a textarea comparison.
